run_notes: The custom `observability-gap-analysis` skill invocation returned `Skill not found` in this environment, so findings were generated by manually following `.github/skills/observability-gap-analysis/SKILL.md`.

findings.job: Get first trace/eval from JS quickly
findings.observed_friction: Arize setup requires explicit OTLP exporter + tracer provider composition and multiple OpenTelemetry/OpenInference packages.
findings.competitor_mechanism: Braintrust exposes `init()` and optional `initLogger()` in one SDK.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Bootstrap path is shorter and avoids direct OTel pipeline wiring for initial setup.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Arize adapter manually creates exporters, span processors, and provider resources before any eval logging.
findings.root_cause: No high-level Arize JS bootstrap that hides low-level OTel plumbing.
findings.proposed_fix: Ship `@arizeai/ax-js` bootstrap helper (`initTracing`) to configure exporter/provider/resources in one call.
findings.success_metric: Time-to-first-trace under 10 minutes and setup code under 20 LOC in a new JS repo.

evidence.note: OpenInference/OpenTelemetry dependency footprint.
Manual OTLP exporter, span processor, and tracer provider setup.
Captured setup friction for package + instrumentation burden.

findings.job: Configure Arize cloud auth without trial-and-error
findings.observed_friction: Cloud path now needs `arize-api-key`, `arize-space-id`, and `ARIZE_PROJECT_NAME`; missing fields fail preflight.
findings.competitor_mechanism: LangSmith client takes API key + optional endpoint URL in one constructor.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Single primary credential pattern reduces auth setup ambiguity.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Arize adapter builds cloud headers manually and switches behavior between local and cloud targets.
findings.root_cause: Arize cloud credential contract diverges from common bearer-key setup and local Arize AX semantics.
findings.proposed_fix: Provide one canonical JS auth contract with strong preflight validation and clearer migration aliases.
findings.success_metric: Credential-related setup retries reduced to one attempt median in onboarding runs.

evidence.note: Notes on `arize-api-key`, `arize-space-id`, and project routing behavior.
Header construction with `ARIZE_SPACE_ID`/`ARIZE_SPACE_ID` aliasing.
Cloud/local env divergence and space key guidance.
Simpler API key + URL client construction.

findings.job: Create datasets consistently for local and cloud setups
findings.observed_friction: Dataset auto-create is attempted only for local Arize AX; cloud flow is separate and manual.
findings.competitor_mechanism: LangSmith offers direct dataset creation in setup (`createDataset`).
findings.why_competitor_easier: A single SDK path handles dataset provisioning as part of bootstrap.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Arize adapter skips cloud dataset provisioning and records a setup note about dashboard/tabular API dependency.
findings.root_cause: No symmetric local/cloud dataset creation helper in Arize JS tooling.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `createDatasetIfMissing` for Arize AX local and Arize cloud with one contract.
findings.success_metric: Dataset-ready state reached by setup code alone in local and cloud modes.

evidence.note: Local-only best-effort dataset creation and cloud skip note.
Recorded asymmetry between local and cloud dataset creation.
Direct dataset creation call in LangSmith setup.

findings.job: Reliably validate Arize AX readiness before eval runs
findings.observed_friction: Arize AX can return 404 on `/healthz` while OTLP ingestion still works, so readiness checks remain ambiguous.
findings.competitor_mechanism: Braintrust ping uses an experiment summarize round-trip call.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Round-trip API checks confirm backend reachability without endpoint-version branching.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Arize ping accepts `404` as potentially healthy and defers certainty to later span delivery checks.
findings.root_cause: Health endpoint behavior differs across Arize AX builds and does not directly prove ingestion.
findings.proposed_fix: Implement a probe-span readiness check that confirms ingestion, not only HTTP endpoint status.
findings.success_metric: Zero false-positive readiness signals across supported Arize AX versions.

evidence.note: 404-on-healthz handling logic.
Friction note on health check ambiguity.
Round-trip summarize call used for readiness.

findings.job: Log eval rows and score summaries without hand-authored span schemas
findings.observed_friction: Arize eval logging requires custom evaluator spans and explicit `evaluation.key.*` attribute naming.
findings.competitor_mechanism: Braintrust row spans can log `input`, `output`, and `scores` directly.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Evaluation data model is first-class in the SDK, reducing schema work in app code.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Run summary and per-issue scores are emitted through manual OpenInference attributes per adapter.
findings.root_cause: No first-class eval-row/eval-summary helper in Arize JS path.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `logEvaluationRow` and `logEvaluationSummary` APIs in Arize JS SDK with defaults for score keys.
findings.success_metric: Per-run eval logging code shrinks by 50% while preserving score fidelity.

evidence.note: Manual evaluator spans and score key attributes.
Gap note on missing first-class eval row helper.
Row score logging via `span.log`.

findings.job: Represent deterministic non-LLM stages with first-class OpenInference semantics
findings.observed_friction: Non-LLM phases are mapped to `CHAIN`, obscuring deterministic-stage semantics in traces.
findings.competitor_mechanism: LangSmith run model captures stage runs with explicit run type and structured input/output payloads.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Deterministic stages can remain structured without inventing custom attributes.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Adapter maps `phase` and fallback kinds to `CHAIN`, then stores deterministic outputs as JSON blobs.
findings.root_cause: OpenInference span-kind taxonomy lacks a dedicated deterministic stage concept.
findings.proposed_fix: Extend OpenInference conventions with deterministic-stage guidance and reference examples.
findings.success_metric: Deterministic stage traces are queryable without adapter-specific custom keys.

evidence.note: Current kind mapping collapses phase/default to `CHAIN`.
Cross-platform note on deterministic-stage awkwardness.
Friction statement on missing clean deterministic span kind.

findings.job: Standardize parent/child trace-tree semantics across SDKs
findings.observed_friction: Equivalent triage -> PM parent/child flow required custom adapter code per platform.
findings.competitor_mechanism: LangSmith run creation accepts explicit `parent_run_id` for hierarchy management.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Tree relationships are explicit in API payloads and easier to reason about in app code.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Cross-platform code translates between OpenInference span kinds, LangSmith run trees, and Braintrust nested spans manually.
findings.root_cause: No portable OpenInference profile defining tree relationship requirements for multi-stage agent pipelines.
findings.proposed_fix: Publish OpenInference trace-tree profile plus conformance tests for parent/child propagation.
findings.success_metric: One canonical parent/child mapping reused across adapters with no per-backend shape forks.

evidence.note: Explicit parent run linkage in LangSmith payload.
Cross-platform mismatch in parent/child modeling.
No shared standard for parent/child traces.

findings.job: Standardize eval-row and summary scoring semantics
findings.observed_friction: OpenInference eval score attachment relies on adapter-chosen attribute names rather than one portable schema.
findings.competitor_mechanism: Braintrust `Eval`/row logging keeps score payloads native to the SDK data model.
findings.why_competitor_easier: Teams can attach scores without inventing key names per project.
findings.current_behavior_in_oss_support_agent: Arize/OpenInference adapter writes custom `evaluation.key.*` fields for row and aggregate metrics.
findings.root_cause: OpenInference semantic conventions do not define a canonical score payload profile for benchmark rows.
findings.proposed_fix: Add OpenInference eval semantic conventions and helper mappers for row + run summary metrics.
findings.success_metric: Cross-platform eval dashboards consume one OpenInference score schema without custom remapping.

evidence.note: Custom `evaluation.key.*` attribute usage.
Braintrust row score payload as first-class object.
Evaluation abstraction differs by platform.
Cross-platform evaluation model mismatch.
